name: Ascent to Pico Teix - Circular from Agres
name_original: Ascenso al Pico Teix — Circular desde Agres
region_intro: Hiking around Agres, located within the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park in Alicante, Spain, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor exploration. The region is characterized by varied terrain, including ancient snow wells known as "cavas," and mountain peaks like Pico Teix. Trails often traverse through natural springs and historical sites, providing a blend of natural beauty and cultural interest. Best hiking trails around Agres The most popular hiking route is Agres Caves Route (SL-CV 168), a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 4 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the area's distinctive cave formations. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ascent to Pico Teix - Circular from Agres, a difficult 4.9 miles (7.9 km) path. This trail features significant elevation gain leading to panoramic views from the summit. Local hikers also love the Cova de l'Habitació – Cava Gran de Agres loop from Agres, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through historical snow wells, often completed in about 3 hours 41 minutes. Hiking around Agres is defined by its location within the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park, ancient "cavas," and varied mountain terrain. The network of trails offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Agres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. Almost 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Agres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Agres?
A: Agres offers a network of over 10 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These routes explore the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Agres?
A: The hiking routes in Agres are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the unique historical features like the 'cavas', and the stunning views from the mountain peaks.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Agres?
A: The Sierra de Mariola Natural Park, where Agres is located, is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Winter hiking is also possible, especially for exploring the ancient snow wells, though some higher routes might require checking local conditions.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Agres?
A: Hiking around Agres features diverse terrain, from paths through pine forests and olive groves to more rugged ascents towards mountain peaks like Pico Teix. Many trails also traverse areas with unique geological formations and historical structures, such as the ancient 'cavas' (snow wells) and natural springs.

Q: Are there hiking options for different skill levels in Agres?
A: Yes, Agres provides hiking options for various abilities. While most routes are considered moderate, there are also easier paths suitable for a more relaxed walk, as well as challenging trails with significant elevation gain for experienced hikers, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868509417/ascent-to-pico-teix-circular-from-agres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Pico Teix - Circular from Agres&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any unique historical features to explore on the trails?
A: Absolutely. Agres is rich in history. Many trails lead to the distinctive ancient snow wells known as 'cavas,' such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14660228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de l'Habitació – Cava Gran de Agres loop from Agres&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find remains of an Arab castle, the Talaia Tower, and archaeological sites like Cabeço de Mariola, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Agres?
A: Yes, several popular circular routes are available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868509417/ascent-to-pico-teix-circular-from-agres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Pico Teix - Circular from Agres&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40375920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cava Gran de Agres – Cava de Don Miguel loop from Agres&lt;/a&gt; explores historical snow wells.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in Agres?
A: Agres offers several trails suitable for families, particularly those looking to discover the rich environment of the Sierra Mariola Natural Park. Many routes are well-suited for a leisurely pace, allowing children to enjoy the natural surroundings and historical sites like the 'cavas'.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Agres?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park, provided they are kept under control, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. It's always advisable to carry water for your pet and clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the diverse flora and fauna of the Sierra de Mariola, you can explore fascinating natural features. The area is known for its numerous natural springs and caves, some with archaeological significance. Hikers can also enjoy stunning panoramic views from various viewpoints and mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4084627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of Pico Teix&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1390751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcabrer Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the hiking trailheads in Agres?
A: While Agres is a small town, public transport options might be limited. It's generally recommended to travel by car to access the various trailheads, as this offers the most flexibility for exploring the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park. Parking is typically available within or near the town.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking in Agres?
A: Parking is usually available within the town of Agres itself, which serves as a common starting point for many routes. Depending on the specific trailhead, you may find designated parking areas or roadside parking options. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.
tips: Also known as the convent of Agres, it is a wonderful place with a lot of tranquility and very nice views of the town. It has a restaurant and parking area.

This is an archaeological site of an old snowfield used to store snow for trade. It is about seven meters in diameter and about ten deep and from here the views are very beautiful. Following a few more meters you can reach the top of the Teix peak.

With its top at 1265 meters above sea level, Pico Teix offers amazing 360 views! You can connect with several very entertaining paths and of different levels of difficulty. Up to here it can be reached by the forest path of l'Ombria in perfect conditions. It deserves to visit it because the views are incredible!
